ref: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
text: Waa ba dɛ biɡri bela tama la, Filisti tena Kooro Abemɛlɛk cheɡɛ tokoro ta, dɛ na kɛŋɔ Aesek ne o haah dɛ hɛlɛ.
translated: The Philistines had not yet invaded the land, and Abimelech drove Isaac away, saying, "Go away from us, for you are much more powerful than we are."

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: dɛ ya jo bwɛɛ nɔ ta ba na n ne hen, dɛ jemɛ kɛŋɔ he jaaree men naa, baa kpo mɛɛ dɛ́ ɡele.
translated: My mother said that if I don't go, she will cry, and I don't want her to cry, so I have to go.

ref: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
text: Belaa ba berɛ kwi onfale, asɛ ba yo o ɡe waa ja. Anee o pa o sɔn Sitna. O munii nɛn nɛɛ cheɡa.
translated: The children are playing outside, their mothers are sitting under the tree. One of them is singing. The others are clapping their hands.
